scenario: System gradually drifts from intended behavior after 30 days of autonomous operation
failure_mode: Allopoietic drift — system optimizes outputs (task completion) but not self-maintenance (architectural coherence)
lesson: Autopoietic systems require operational closure: every process must either produce system components or enable production. If KAIROS produces improvements that bypass Iron Dome, autopoiesis breaks.
counterfactual: Regular autopoietic audits: is every KAIROS proposal routed through all 5 VSM subsystems? Are improvements maintaining the boundary (identity) of the system?
prevention: Weekly VSM audit checking all 5 subsystems are healthy and interconnected. Track ratio: self-generated improvements / externally-triggered responses.
